merger_name: National Dental Care – business assets comprising the Cambridge Dental Centre clinic, Perth, WA
merger_description:
NDC HoldCo Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, **National Dental Care**), through its wholly-owned subsidiary, NDC Australia Pty Limited, proposes to acquire from the Camdent Services Unit Trust 100% of the business assets comprising the Cambridge Dental Centre (**Cambridge Dental**).

National Dental Care is a dental services organisation that operates a network of dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry services. National Dental Care has dental clinics in all mainland Australian states and territories, including 26 clinics in Perth, WA.

Cambridge Dental is a dental clinic located in Perth, WA that provides a range of general dentistry services

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
NDC Australia Pty Limited (National Dental Care) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the assets comprising the Cambridge Dental Centre from Camdent
Services Unit Trust, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, National Dental Care, is a dental services organisation
that operates a network of dental clinics that provide a range of
general dentistry services. National Dental Care has dental clinics in
all mainlands Australian states and territories, including 26 clinics in
WA. National Dental Care is majority owned by funds managed by
Crescent Capital Partners
Cambridge Dental Centre is a dental clinic located in Perth, WA that
provides a range of general dentistry services.
National Dental Care and Cambridge Dental Centre both supply
general dentistry services in Perth, WA.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the parties’ combined share in the number of clinics
supplying general dentistry services in the area surrounding
Cambridge Dental Centre is low
b. there are alternative suppliers of general dentistry services in
Perth, WA.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
